intro: Krenakia, also known as Baccharis genistelloides, is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Krenakia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal preparations, Krenakia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
